patches.hypothesis: The method enters an infinite loop when the calculated wrap position never changes because the text length exceeds the specified width, resulting in the same wrap position and throwing a runtime exception. Fix involves breaking long words when they exceed the given width.
patches.changes: - Added a check to prevent infinite loop by breaking long words longer than 'width' + for text that doesn't contain spaces to enable wrapping.
patches.diff: - Added a check to prevent infinite loop by breaking long words longer than 'width' + for text that doesn't contain spaces to enable wrapping.

patches.hypothesis: The previous solution was overfitting by handling specific conditions that forced the loop to break unnecessarily. The root cause was not effectively addressed, leading to an ungeneralized solution. The refined patch should properly handle word wrapping by ensuring the padding and position calculations balance text offset properly, allowing for a natural and logical break in text wrapping without needing forced loop breaks.
patches.original_hypothesis: The method runs into an infinite loop when the text after wrapping is still too long for the specified width. This happens because the method re-appends padding to the text, which makes any further wrap position unusable. The fix involves adjusting the logic to account for cases where padding causes the text to remain too long.

patches.hypothesis: The previous patch adjusted the behavior of findWrapPos by updating the starting position parameter based on nextLineTabStop, which can lead to incorrect line wrapping especially for edge cases like long words or no spaces. To address this, ensure correct initial word wrapping without both affecting future padding and handling edge cases properly.
patches.original_hypothesis: The method throws an exception when a wrapped text line exceeds the allowed width limit repeatedly, leading to an infinite loop prevention. The issue stems from the padding being applied improperly when calculating new wrap positions.

patches.hypothesis: The method throws an exception when it encounters text that exactly matches or exceeds the wrap width and can't be processed correctly, leading to the same position being found repeatedly, causing an infinite loop.
patches.changes: +add check for pos and lastPos equality and break loop, append last section correctly
patches.diff: +add check for pos and lastPos equality and break loop, append last section correctly

patches.hypothesis: The issue arises when the substring of text to wrap is equal to the width, causing it to trim entirely, resulting in identical pos values, leading to an infinite loop. This fix ensures the starting position of the next line is adjusted so that a valid next wrap point is found.
patches.changes: -        pos = findWrapPos(text, width, 0);  +        pos = findWrapPos(text, width, nextLineTabStop);
patches.diff: -        pos = findWrapPos(text, width, 0);  +        pos = findWrapPos(text, width, nextLineTabStop);
